reasoning: The forecast begins at a local low of 1.30% for the 5-year TIPS yield on Friday, 2025-11-21. This was a sharp 4-basis-point drop from the previous day's 1.34%, driven by dovish comments from Fed official John Williams regarding a potential December rate cut. Because 1.30 represents the trough for the month of November (recent modal values were in the 1.34–1.35% range), basic regression to the mean and short-term volatility suggest a high probability that the yield will retrace upward by the end of November. The FRED statistical tool gives an expected short-term probability of an increase around 69-71% based on momentum and volatility metrics. As the forecast horizon extends to late December and February, greater macroeconomic uncertainty (including actual Fed rate decisions and broader economic data) allows standard random walk dynamics to dominate, meaning the impact of the current anomalous starting point diminishes. Accordingly, the probability of an increase remains favorable but moderates progressively closer to the 0.50 base rate for the longer-term resolution dates.
